sanskrit:
एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च।
कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्चितं मतमुत्तमम्॥
transliteration:
etāny api tu karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā phalāni ca
kartavyānīti me pārtha niścitaṃ matam uttamam
literal_meaning: These actions indeed, O Pārtha, abandoning attachment and fruits, are to be done; this is My definite, highest teaching.
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a final, crystal-clear directive: act because it is right, not because it will feel good or look good.  Drop the inner score-keeper that tallies gains, losses, praise or blame.  The mind is to be re-purposed from outcome-seeking to value-alignment.
psychological_state: Post-decisional calm with residual performance anxiety; ego threatened by loss of measurable success, yet Self stabilizes around intrinsic values.
trigger_scenario: You are up for promotion.  Colleagues play politics, but your integrity says finish the project well and let the chips fall.  Heart races, yet you code through the night without checking email for praise.
anchor_text: Do what is right, then let go of the outcome.
